SUBJECT: Короткая идея по UX для вашей платформы
CONTENT:
Ассалому алейкум ва рахматуллохи ва баракатуху! ,
Рамадан муборак, Александр Константинов,

Постараюсь очень кратко — всего 2 минуты вашего времени.

Я познакомилась с вашим направлением в области целостных систем, осознанности и сотрудничества, и искренне восхищена глубиной подхода. Такие проекты важно не просто объяснять — их нужно ощущать через опыт взаимодействия.

Меня зовут Севараксон Абдухалилова, CEO и Senior UX/UI Designer.
Я помогаю фаундерам с визионерскими продуктами превращать сложные идеи в понятный, логичный и интуитивный пользовательский путь.

Часто в подобных проектах возникают скрытые сложности:
— Сильная идея, но абстрактный онбординг
— Понятен «зачем», но неочевидно «что делать дальше»
— Сайт не передаёт глубину проекта с первого экрана

Именно здесь я могу быть полезна.

Если вы не против, я бы с удовольствием посмотрела один из ваших ключевых цифровых тачпоинтов (лендинг или онбординг) и отправила 3 конкретных UX-улучшения, которые можно внедрить сразу. Это бесплатно и займёт не больше 2 минут с вашей стороны.

Если откликается — просто пришлите ссылку на страницу, которая для вас сейчас наиболее важна.

С уважением,
Севараксон Абдухалилова
CEO & Senior UX/UI Designer

У меня нет взгляда на «борьбу со злом». Я считаю что это навязанная программа.

Я предпочитаю не бороться когда есть другие способы - несопротивление (ахимса, айкидо). Не бездействие! А несопротивление. 

Борьба создает ответную борьбу, перетягивание каната заходит все дальше, чем больше борьбы. 

Борьба - устаревшая стратегия, люди могут ее превзойти и выбрать другие способы улучшения ситуации которые не разжигают энергию конфликта, а лишают его топлива, не подливая его. 

Понятно, что если нападет хулиган, подставлять три раза щеку неумно и нужно защититься, но совсем другое дело выбирать при этом все большее вовлечение в конфликт обосновывая острой необходимостью «борьбы со злом», без способности нормально определить что за борьба, и с каким именно злом без использования готовых шаблонов из масс-медиа и прочих распространяемых готовых нарративов.

А «зло» - на мой взгляд миф, не абсолютного зла. Есть невежество в разной степени и в разных проявлениях.

На мой взгляд оптимальная точка импакта - увеличение качества сознания и восприятия людей на планете оптимальными способами.

SUBJECT: The Stoic Startup
CONTENT:
Hey Aleksandr, I'm reaching out as fellow founder. Good to connect. I'm a marketer by heart, and I love growing companies that have a deep drive.

So next to building companies, I love teaching / guiding as well. And especially on topics which overlap mental strength, resilience, and ultimately both success and a meaningful life as a founder. Maven asked me recently to launch a new online course, and The Stoic Startup is coming very soon <3

Stoic? Yes. In todays world stoicism is seen as a bit negative, emotionless maybe even. But the opposite is true when it comes to startups. It's the stoic founder who is certain about the future, can handle both immediate success or failure, and has a clear vision and mission, both personally and for the company.

In the course we will focus on discovering these insights from the philosophy of stoicism and how to apply it as a founder. I'm attaching a few slides to check it out, and would love for you to join. Enrollment is not live yet, but you can join the waiting list here (no strings attached obv): https://maven.com/forms/fa1915

У меня все хорошо, сейчас у нас в Мексике переходный период - согласно недавно принятной конституциональной реформе в области телекоммуникаций, наш регулятор уже буквально всего через месяц сменит название, статус, а также получит больший мандат, увеличит бюджет, количество работников и т.д. Соотвественно, грядут интересные перемены, но вроде пока что исключительно в позитивном свете. Мой план пока работать здесь, но начинать разведывать уже территорию, чтобы через примерно год уже двигаться дальше. Еще из новостей - мексиканский проект по созданию open-access nationwide wholesale LTE network идет полным ходом. Не знаю, слышали ли Вы, в конце июня Минкомсвязи объявил, что они намереваются построить аналогичную сеть по абсолютно идентичной модели. Более, того, уже есть какая-то модель, так как в Ведомостях были уже цифры основные (2 млрд долларов стоимости и примерно 30 000 БС). Цифры у меня, как инсайдера такого проекта в Мексике, честно говоря, вызывают недоумение. С тех пор новостей не было, и вот я гадаю, что же там происходит.

Сама реформа на удивление качественна, продумана и вся динамика очень позитивна. Более того, вся работа нового правительства происходит на каком-то невиданном по качеству уровне - уже несколько очень нужных и хорошо сделанных реформ, очень компетентные и нацеленные на результат люди в команде президента и в новом правительстве. Когда мы увидели текст реформы, который держался в тайне до презентации, Луис сказал - "что я могу сказать по поводу реформы? Я могу только поаплодировать." :)

Что касается Вашего вопроса: 

1. Буквально недавно Кофетель принял резолюцию именно для rural communities (не только для коренных жителей) о том, что в населенных пунктах с кол-вом жителей не больше 2500 разрешается использование устройств в полосе 2.4 ГГц с бОльшим радиусом действия, чем обычные 50-100 метров обычных wifi роутеров. Я не на 100% уверен только ли идет речь о гос. проектах (какие-то community centers, передающие траффик через спутниковую связь, и "расшеривающие" ее с охватом большей части населенного пункта) или же нет никаких ограничений. Могу поискать в этой резолюции. 

2. Была еще пара инициатив такого рода, как то, что делает Ваш знакомый, но там проблема была в том, что оборудование, которое хотели использовать, было бы слишком дорогим из-за отсутствия серийного производства. 

3. Вообще, такие проекты любой гос. орган более чем поддерживает подобные инициативы. Пусть Ваш товарищ с нами связывается, поможем и поддержим. Есть такая Monica Aspe, которая координирует гос. инициативы в этой области, возможно с ней тоже можно было бы встретиться. В общем, пусть со мной свяжется, встретится, расскажет и посмотрим, что можно сделать.

Как у вас дела? Вы ушли из ITU? 

Саша

Would something like this be an option or would you be open to learning more?

CONTENT:
Hi Sasha,

I am the Business Development Associate of Fruit Street Health, a telemedicine company that is owned by more than 500 physicians & angel investors who have invested more than $24 million into building our company over the last seven years.

Fruit Street delivers the CDC's diabetes prevention program via telehealth and live group video conferencing. We are now expanding into other telemedicine services such as virtual primary care, behavioral health, and other virtual chronic disease management programs that use group visits.

Fruit Street is currently recruiting advisors for our company. One of the company’s benefits we offer to advisors is that they have the opportunity to invest and many do so via their IRA/401K. This is not required to be on the advisory board, but we like to see our advisors have skin in the game.

This is nicely summarizes our company’s history: https://medcitynews.com/2020/06/cultivated-by-physician-investors-fruit-street-healths-evolution-as-a-telehealth-business/

To learn more about our diabetes prevention program that has Full CDC Recognition, watch this video: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=rTujudQ_-zE&t=0s

This is a video about our virtual primary care product we are launching: https://www.dropbox.com/s/4ezqn6wyhbtp0zk/zoom_2.mp4?dl=0

You can connect with our CEO on LinkedIn here: https://www.linkedin.com/in/girardlaurence/. Here is our pitch deck which provides a nice summary of our company for potential advisory board members: https://www.clearslide.com/view/mail?iID=xtRXM9cSQWQN7tFS8hwb

Here are a couple media interviews our CEO just did:
https://bit.ly/3tL9AbY
https://bit.ly/3fdHKA0

Our company is growing quickly and we are projecting to do between $5-6 million in revenue this year. Last year we received a $60M acquisition offer which was 30x our revenue. Please let me know if you are interested in speaking.

Best Regards, 

James Bastian Palos
Business Development Associate 
Fruit Street Health

Muchas gracias por aceptar mi invitación. En algun lado vi el concepto de Personalytics y realmente me llamó la atención. En las últimas semanas hemos estado tratando de entender mas sobre los cambios que esta pandemia va a dejar en los sistemas de salud de todo el mundo. Creo que nos ha hecho ver muchas cosas como el hecho  de que los sistemas de salud no tienen la capacidad de atención de toda la población ante situaciones como estas y que el empoderamiento de los pacientes para ser partícipes de su propio cuidado es cada vez más relevante. Esto ya lo venía yo viendo desde antes por diferentes razones, entre ellas el cambio en los modelos de financiamiento de salud en los que, para las instituciones, el paciente enfermo que hasta ahora ha sido el más rentable será ahora el menos (Shift from Fee for service to Fee per Capita). Esto motiva a las mismas instituciones y sistemas de seguros a habilitar a los pacientes para tomar cada vez mas medidas preventivas y de estilo de vida. La integración de AI a la interpretación e los datos y el agregar la data de diferentes plataformas que ya están sembradas en la población (wearables y home devices) es un home run. Dear Alexander,

As 10EQS is now approaching the 1,500-project mark, I want to give you a quick update on four new capabilities that may be of interest to you:

• Increasingly, our work involves "doing", not just "analyzing". For example, we will not only analyze a market for a product in a specific country, we will also deliver the names, contact information and decision criteria of buyers within that market. We can then arrange meetings with potential buyers for you, and we can even support your bidding and negotiation teams with industry/technology experts in meetings as needed.

• As innovation is becoming ever more important, we can now organize challenge competitions for you, tapping into the global community of millions of creative solvers to support you in product development and operational improvement. We can create a customized website that carries your challenge details and allows you to follow progress continuously. 

• In case you need details on markets, customers, competitors, or regulations in more difficult-to-reach geographies, we have developed an extensive "ground game" in most African, Asian or Latin American countries that would be available for supporting your local business.

• Given our recruitment capabilities in the global professional crowd, many clients have asked us to support their teams through staff augmentation and recruitment - so we have added these services to our offerings.

In addition to the above, we continue to offer a broad range of crowd-based solutions, working online on our proprietary platform with custom-recruited teams of subject-matter specialists that "have been there, done that". You can access all services on our website www.10EQS.com. We respond to inquiries within 1 hour, and we assemble virtual teams within 24 hours.

Have a look at our most recent overview at https://www.10eqs.com/about_us#presentation

Best,

Maxim

Maxim Kalmykov
Managing Director, Russia & CIS
Maxim.Kalmykov@10EQS.com

PS.

Just as a quick add-on to the prior communication, let me share with you some details on the product that is currently popular among our clients – online expert workshops.

These allow our clients to discuss directly with the experts we find and vet the topics of their most interest and concern, be that market entry, product development, technology implementation or just ideas generation/brainstorming around the client’s problem.

The structure of these workshops is very simple: “3+2”, meaning 3 introductory 60-90 minutes workshops + 2 optional deep-dives. The price structure is simple too: EUR 7K and EUR 3K pre-paid separately, EUR 10K in total.

As a result of the workshops the client obtains detailed interview notes and an online recording (if experts agree). The workshops are conducted in a facilitated online video environment.

Hope this raises interest in trying 10EQS, as the approach not only allows to cost-efficiently address most critical issues quickly and relatively cheaply, but also is a demo of how 10EQS works!

Would be happy to discuss this in more detail!

Hi Alexander, 

Sorry for my very late response - I have been out of office the past two weeks as I had a family emergency to tend to. 

Essentially, Neudata speak to providers of alt data, like yourself, and we aim to understand key details of your dataset. We act impartially on behalf of our buyside clients who pay us a fee to access our company's take on the wider data market and data scouting services. We speak with large and small name data providers to help our clients find new and exciting sources for their investment strategy.

Both of your data sources sound extremely interesting, especially as we are working on expanding our coverage for Latin American data - which I am sure you are aware is not widely available.

It would be great if we could schedule a call to discuss further if this would be of interest to you. I will gladly dive deeper into what it is we do at Neudata, as well.

Thanks,
Jose

Hi Alexander, 

I am currently recruiting for the following, please let me know if it's of interest.

Senior Director, Business Development urgently required for exciting position within a global pre-IPO disruptive technology company which is expanding rapidly. My client is one of the fastest growing technology unicorns with a strong global presence. This position will be based in either the Bay Area or Texas. 

The successful candidate will have significant experience in business leadership, general management  or management consulting roles, with a proven ability to deliver results. This will include client-facing experience ideally of a commercial / business development / sales nature. Experience in detailed analytics and data-driven decision making. You must thrive in a fast-paced, high-growth environment.
 
Responsibilities:
- Be the strategic leader and driver of increased revenue across your region (only 6 regions in the US and you will run one of them) - functional team of 200-300 with a number of direct reports.
- Build a team of passionate, dedicated individuals - including Finance, Revenue Projection and Contracts - to deliver on the core growth metric.
- Set up deal desk at each City in your region to conduct deal reviews with the goal to unblock deals, thus increasing deal velocity.
- Liaise with the Region team to maintain the pipeline tracking and forecasting.
- Subject matter expert in structuring deals, driving sales process, and training and enabling sales teams.
- Synthesize and communicate achievements and plan for senior leadership on a weekly basis.

This is a genuine opportunity to join a game-changing market-leader that will change the industry they operate in.

Salary: $150k - $200k base + Generous Bonus + Stock + Benefits

Please reply to learn more. Urgent!

Best,

 Leo Wilkins
Director at Wilkins Hunter

Hi Alexander,

Greetings!

I am a Recruiter at Qubole and found your profile impressive and a great match for Account Executive - core accounts position based out of our Santa Clara office.

Pls let me know if you are keen exploring opportunities with us. If so, lets arrange for sometime to have a quick chat.

About Qubole - 

Qubole has built a cutting edge, enterprise grade big data platform for the cloud that processes more than 750 PB of data every month and is used by 70+ companies like Pinterest, Lyft, Shazam, Thomson Reuters, Autodesk etc. to power their big data analytics. Qubole SaaS offering is a Self-Service Big Data Analytics Platform which runs on leading cloud offerings like Amazon Web Services, Google Compute and Microsoft Azure. 

Our founders are the creators of Apache Hive and the people who built out Facebook's Data Infrastructure and have pioneered a lot of innovation in the Big Data Ecosystem. We are a venture backed company and our investors are top tier Silicon Vally venture firms - Lightspeed, CRV and Norwest.

Hey! Nice to meet you Aizaz!
I am a part of ProductCamp Russia & Eastern Europe (EE) team, which is the largest product management community in EE so far. We are organizing the best product management events in this region since 2009. It's free, cool, fun and very useful.
I’d like to invite you to join the ProductCamp to share your experience as a speaker for SKYPE Q&A SESSION at the 28 September at 12 00 PM Pacific Time Zone
ProductCamp is a place where product people meet to share their failures and celebrate successes. You will have a chance to meet product managers, CEOs, founders, marketers and analysts from companies such as Google, Yandex, Gett, Wrike, VK,  Mail.ru, Tinkoff, Wargaming, Parallels, Skyeng, Brainly, Zeptolab, Coub iFunny, ManyChat, Chatfuel, and many more. We've organized the event for 9 years now and we are trying to engage more people from different countries into the community.
The key value of ProductCamp is the fact that each member contributes to making this event great and puts a heart and soul into doing so. Camp program is a mix of presentations, workshops and round tables organized and facilitated by participants. So, the atmosphere is very cozy and empowering.
So, what's in it for you?:
• You will get a chance to network face-to-face with more than 200 product managers, CEOs, and founders from the most successful product companies in Russia and Eastern Europe as well you will be warmly welcomed by more than 5700 community members.
• You will have an opportunity to immerse yourself in an atmosphere of the camp which is created by the community. So, you'll get a little more than other professional events usually offer. This is achieved because each Camp participant contributes to its preparation and implementation. You'll also become a part of it! For example, you can contribute to Camp preparation by forwarding this invitation to your colleagues, partners, and friends.
• Last but not least, you will get an opportunity to 'taste' a Slavic culture to its fullest. It's going to be fun, we promise!
What do you think? Do you want to join on this event?

SUBJECT: JIG-SAW Inc. Japan (North American expansion)
CONTENT:
Hi Alexander, 

Are you interested in a joining our global expansion venture in the IoT space?

We would like to invite you to an interview to discuss your experience and the possibility of joining our North American team based in California.
 
We are looking for a skilled and personable individual who can handle our strategic business development, create partnerships and expand our platform in the IoT industrial space. (e,g smart city, logistics, cold chain, lighting and more)
 
In short, this is a Business Development position to support our aggressive global expansion ambition to bring our “neqto” platform from Japan into the US market.
 
 
About JIG-SAW US:
 
JIG-SAW US is a start-up establishment with co-offices in Santa Monica and San Francisco. With satellite establishment in Toronto and Vancouver.
 
Our parent company JIG-SAW Inc. [ https://www.jig-saw.com/en/  ] is headquartered in Tokyo and is an established leader in IoT solutions in Japan. Listed in the Tokyo exchange (stock ticker 3914).
 
JIG-SAW overall provides an IoT service “neqto” [ https://neqto.com/en/ ] and our global expansion resulted in the creation of JIG-SAW US to pursue our growth in the North American market.
 
General Roles, Responsibilities & Expectations:
 
- Collaborate our Product Head and Marketing partner as well as work with our global strategic partners, Cloud Vendors, SaaS Providers, System Integrators, AI, and the Machine Learning Players.
- Demonstrate a passion for future of the IoT and aggressively seek new partnership with strategic values to our North American expansion. This also involves creating use cases & proof of concept trials with the partners found.
- Travel within NA is required - based on Business Development efforts.

If you are interested, please do reply and we can setup a time to discuss. gavin.liaw@jig-saw.com

Thank you.

 Gavin Liaw
Sr. Director and Leadership Team at JIG-SAW US (San Francisco Bay Area)

SUBJECT: Opportunity to join Vertoe as a Senior Partnerships Manager & Account Executive!
CONTENT:
Dear Alexander, 

My name is Candice, I’m a Talent Development Manager at Planted. We’re a smart career platform that matches top business talent with growing companies. 

I think you’d be a great fit as a Senior Partnerships Manager & Account Executive with Vertoe! 

Vertoe is America’s first and largest on-demand, short-term storage provider with 300+ locations across all major cities in the U.S. They’re marketplace connects anyone looking for short-term storage, from a few hours or days, to local shops and businesses that have space. 

They’re a young, seed-funded ($1.8 million) startup that’s growing quickly and graduated from the winter 2018 program of world’s #2 ranked startup accelerator, Techstars in NYC (one of the 11 selected startups out of 1600+ who applied). The Vertoe team consists of folks who have worked at Google, Microsoft, Lindt, MetLife, AIG, and Penguin Random House in the past.

In this role, you'll lead Vertoe's company-wide partnership efforts, including but not limited to creating pitch materials, sizing and modeling partnership impact for their clients, and leading meetings with CXOs. You’ll also receive great benefits, including stock options in Vertoe!

To Apply, review the job description and full salary details, (located in the Q&A portion of the application, up to $80K base + commission) please head here: 

https://go.planted.com/VertoeSPMAE

Let me know if you do apply so I can review your application, since Vertoe is looking to interview ASAP! 

If you could send me a quick LI message either way and let me know if you’re interested in any new opportunities, I’d appreciate it. We can also open up other roles Planted has to offer with many different companies & industries once you visit our platform here:  https://go.planted.com/SpecialPlantedInvite 

Thank you,

Candice

Or …
You’re part of the industry. It’s not part of your official job description, but you’re always selling—to colleagues, clients, CEOs. And you’re a natural. You’d like a paycheck that reflects that.

Very competitive salary

Generous retirement savings plan
Ample time off
Continuous training and development opportunities
Service recognition awards

About Our Company
We are a premier staffing agency delivering advertising, marketing, creative, and interactive talent solutions to businesses ranging from startups to Fortune 100 corporations. We represent some of the industry’s most talented individuals, and our company is growing along with our reputation.
 
About the Position
As a Sales Account Executive, your role is to forge and foster productive partnerships for Creative Circle. Your mission: grow existing relationships and fearlessly go after new business. You work tirelessly to understand each client’s needs and to deliver Creative Circle as the ideal solution. With our premium brand and your innate drive, you open doors, close sales and actually make a difference for your clients.
 
Your motivation: the direct financial gain that comes with each of your successes. At Creative Circle commissions are uncapped. Your earning potential is unlimited.
 
As an empowered member of a collaborative team, you thrive on healthy competition in a fast-paced, high-volume office buzzing with creative energy.
 
Now, About You
Ideally, you have least three years of experience in advertising or marketing plus a background in sales. You’re mature, motivated and ready to achieve the next level of career success.

SUBJECT: Intro Call re: Data Partnership
CONTENT:
Hi Alexander,

Hope this note finds you well. I wanted to see if you'd be open to a brief 15 minute intro call to determine if there's a fit for a data partnership.

With Grandata's ability to leverage big data to identify key market trends and predict customer interactions I think there are several relevant use cases for Unacast's alternative data.

In brief, Unacast is the largest aggregator of location and proximity data. We ingest SDK-level signals, make sense of the raw data and then license out cleansed data to companies to fuel their various products and solutions.

We can provide the data as visits (with POI attached to commercial venues) or as pure data (no POI). 

Would you be open to a 15 minute call next Thurs Dec 7th some time between 9am - 1pm PST to determine together if there might be a fit and grounds for a larger discussion?

Best,
Jessica

So, I am in San Francisco since November last year, working for a startup in big data and we actually work with retail banks. So I was thinking: if you do any digital / innovative stuff as part of your job it would probably be interesting for you to look at what we do. Or if not, maybe you could make an intro to someone relevant at Amex, normally digital / marketing / customer analytics people get excited about the innovative stuff we do. Here are a few lines of what we do:

We actually do stuff that nobody else in the US does, we work with some of the world's global Top-10 banks and we started to work with 2 banks in the US (a.k.a. the first adult B2B contracts I signed in my life hahaha). AT&T gives much of their data exclusively to us and then we combine it with bank's data by matching profiles though phone number. We then do data analytics that actually came from MIT research (we build social graph of the US population, study geolocation and mobility patterns  of people etc) and thus see stuff about the existing customers of the bank hidden to the bank (for example, if the bank is primary or secondary for a customer; or when somebody just moved or changed jobs for the payroll or mortgage to be offered to that person), as well as the info on the open market such as filtering prospects for a consumer loan or premium credit card with high propensity for acquisition. And many more things like that.

Let me know what you think and have a great day!
Alex

Hi Alexander, 

I hope this message finds you well! I came across your profile and thought you’d make a great candidate for The Broad Residency in Urban Education.

In case you’re not familiar, The Broad Residency is an opportunity for socially conscious professionals to leverage their business experience towards improving our country’s education system. We've had really great residents with profiles similar to yours in the past (you can see them here: http://www.broadcenter.org/residency/about/residency-network?/network/profiles) and I thought you might be interested in learning more.

I've copied a brief overview of the program below--feel free to take a look. You've clearly done some impressive work and I imagine our nation’s school systems would really benefit from the management experience and perspective you hold.  

If you are interested in applying, we'll be accepting resumes until January 20, 2017. (You can submit here: http://www.broadcenter.org/residency/join/resume-submission). Also--if you have any friends or colleagues that might be interested, I'd appreciate if you could pass this information along. Let me know if you have any questions!

Best,

Sarah Thompson
sthompson@broadcenter.org
www.managementthatmatters.org

The Broad Residency is a 2-year leadership development program that matches talented professionals (most of whom have NOT worked in education previously) with management roles in public education agencies throughout the nation. These high impact, meaningful roles have starting salaries of $90,000-100,000. In addition to their full-time jobs, Broad Residents participate in a rigorous professional development program in which they come to understand the context of urban education and how best to leverage their skills towards improving the lives of students

 For more information: http://managementthatmatters.org/

Dear Alex:

Hi Julie! I just realized I never responded to your message, it got lost in my mailbox, my apologies for that :( To answer your questions, I really really enjoyed my time at Sloan and I think with time I will be appreciate it more and more. All you said during our interview 2.5 years ago on what was waiting for me at Sloan was very very true. Thank you very much for believing in me back then, I really got what I wanted from this MBA experience - passing on to a next level intellectually and investing into my own very brain and cognition - in addition to everything else obviously.

After graduation, I went back to Mexico and did a 2.5 months "post-MBA internship" project through MISTI at a North American airline based in Mexico (Volaris). It was great, yesterday was actually my last day. They liked my work and offered both an extension and a full-time position as the head of customer analytics (that was one of my three projects there). They don't pay well (neither for the internship not full-time) at all so I am going to reject their offer and work on the startup of my ex-boss but it is also temporary and for little money too but with a chance of getting some good money if it works out. I had two good offers in the fall - Samsung Global Strategy (in Korea..) and AT Kearney in Mexico. I decided to reject them both a look for a dream job and in a location that I want to be in - either London or SF/NYC. Tough to get ones as you can see... Since then, I figured out that I was mostly interested (and have expertise) in telecom data analytics so I am looking for a full-time job in this domain mostly. I am talking to 1) startups in this field; 2) innovation / digital divisions of telcos and related companies; 3) strategy/consulting/partnerships roles within broader internet / tech companies. As of now, it is a work in progress, it has been much tougher than I thought...

What about you? It isn't clear from your LinkedIn profile if you are still engaged with the ABS or you are moving on to something else. What follows? 

Have a great weekend,
Alex

Hi Robert!

I hope you are doing great! How is UNGP, what have you guys been up to?

I just wanted to share some updates on my side. I graduated last month and I am now doing a 2 month project in the Strategy department of an airline in Mexico called Volaris. Alexander explored and successfully presented on the following areas around big data and climate action: 1. Rain Fade: A precipitation measurement mechanism based on the rain fade effect. 2. Raytracing: Exposure mapping using radio signal attenuation for the insurance industry. 3. Early warning systems: Increasing availability of early warning of natural hazards based on cell broadcast technology. 4. Self-Organizing Networks: Potential usage of mobile networks for faster recovery after natural hazards to warn about landslides and, as well as for energy savings in mobile networks. This reference is being presented to Alexander with thanks for his hard work and diligence throughout the course of the programme. Not only has he expanded his thought process on the concepts related to big data and climate action, but also demonstrated ability in delivering results beyond expectations and putting theory into practice. 

Thank you!
Alex

General information on such positions from my experience:
1. They are very few in number at any company that I have looked into. These teams tend to be small. I actually did submit a Sloan fellows resume for the Director position, and I heard from an executive that they are receiving too much interest both internally and externally.
2. If you are particularly looking into M&A and investments, it is even harder. A lot of these positions tend to fill through word of mouth.
3. That said, I am not trying to discourage at all, but stating the facts. Keep at it, sooner or later something will work out given your background and experience.
4. I would also highly encourage applying to mid-sized companies or startups with decent funding for such similar positions.
5. In terms of interviews, these have varied from company to company, team to team, role to role, so I can't just say do case studies or have general industry knowledge. These two will help and resume, experience questions too.

If I see appropriate positions anywhere, I will make sure to forward. Please let me know if you have any other questions. I hope this helps. Good luck!

Best,
Esha

I am in NYC so I won't be in Boston in the next couple of weeks for sure unfortunately. 

Let me give you a little bit more detail on my question: I am working on a project where we would like to automatically identify the material of construction of buildings in urban areas in a city (mainly in developing countries) with the help of RF planning tools and telecom maps that would help to infer what a building is made of by observing the attenuation of the signal as it goes through the walls. The idea is to have it done automatically without manual inspection and give this data to firefighters and also to insurance companies. Currently, insurers cannot give insurance for buildings on a large scale in urban areas in developing countries because they don't know how resistant to floods and storms. If insurance companies have this data, they will enter more markets and will be able to insure buildings in that area. Then, when there a flood or a storm, the city would be much quicker and stronger to recover because it was insured. Do you think it is feasible to use RF planning software (like Forsk's Atoll, or mPlanet) for this, let's say, unusual purpose without doing the field tests? 

I hope I am making myself clearer this time..Let me know if this is still unclear.

Alex

Hi Alexander

I hope you are well. We spoke briefly at the ITU meeting in Mexico City last year, our Director Robert gave a talk there about Big Data.

I'm writing to let you know about a short consultancy coming up here in the UN. I understand that you yourself have commitments but I thought that you might know of someone who is suitable.

We are seeking expressions of interest from individual researchers/consultants interested in working with Global Pulse & ITU on a potential project aiming to uncover new methods and data sources for measuring global internet penetration.

A description of the project can be found online at this link (http://bit.ly/U7g4Sf).

In summary, the scope of work would include researching feasible technical options, and bringing together a consortium of private sector partners to work on a pilot project with a proposed workplan.

The ideal candidate would be have a dual skill-set:

(i) experience/ability to understand the technical and informational aspects of internet and mobile data infrastructure, data storage and transfer as well as issues surrounding privacy.
(ii) some experience in technology or technology policy consulting; in particular the ideal candidate will be comfortable with interviewing and project planning with private sector partners.

The duration of engagement is expected to be about 90 working days over the course of June-November 2014, and the would be “home-based” with occasional travel as required.

Please feel free to share with appropriate networks and encourage qualified people to email roster@unglobalpulse.org with their CVs and a brief statement of interest.

Thanks and best!

Alex

Dear Sir,

We, M/s. e-Procurement Technologies Limited (ETL), introduce ourselves as an ISO 9001 & ISO 27001 certified Organization involved in providing e-procurement and e-auction solution more than 100 clienteles, both in the Government and Corporate sector. With headquarters based at Ahmedabad, India, our e-procurement suite, www.abcprocure.com, constitute the entire gamut of procurement consisting of Indent, e-Tendering, e-RF(x), e-Auctions, and Contract Management module, which has been used by our clienteles’ to conduct e-procurement worth USD 25 Billion.

Здравствуйте Юрий!

У меня все хорошо, сейчас у нас в Мексике переходный период - согласно недавно принятной конституциональной реформе в области телекоммуникаций, наш регулятор уже буквально всего через месяц сменит название, статус, а также получит больший мандат, увеличит бюджет, количество работников и т.д. Соотвественно, грядут интересные перемены, но вроде пока что исключительно в позитивном свете. Мой план пока работать здесь, но начинать разведывать уже территорию, чтобы через примерно год уже двигаться дальше. Еще из новостей - мексиканский проект по созданию open-access nationwide wholesale LTE network идет полным ходом. Не знаю, слышали ли Вы, в конце июня Минкомсвязи объявил, что они намереваются построить аналогичную сеть по абсолютно идентичной модели. Более, того, уже есть какая-то модель, так как в Ведомостях были уже цифры основные (2 млрд долларов стоимости и примерно 30 000 БС). Цифры у меня, как инсайдера такого проекта в Мексике, честно говоря, вызывают недоумение. С тех пор новостей не было, и вот я гадаю, что же там происходит.

Сама реформа на удивление качественна, продумана и вся динамика очень позитивна. Более того, вся работа нового правительства происходит на каком-то невиданном по качеству уровне - уже несколько очень нужных и хорошо сделанных реформ, очень компетентные и нацеленные на результат люди в команде президента и в новом правительстве. Когда мы увидели текст реформы, который держался в тайне до презентации, Луис сказал - "что я могу сказать по поводу реформы? Я могу только поаплодировать." :)

Что касается Вашего вопроса: 

1. Буквально недавно Кофетель принял резолюцию именно для rural communities (не только для коренных жителей) о том, что в населенных пунктах с кол-вом жителей не больше 2500 разрешается использование устройств в полосе 2.4 ГГц с бОльшим радиусом действия, чем обычные 50-100 метров обычных wifi роутеров. Я не на 100% уверен только ли идет речь о гос. проектах (какие-то community centers, передающие траффик через спутниковую связь, и "расшеривающие" ее с охватом большей части населенного пункта) или же нет никаких ограничений. Могу поискать в этой резолюции. 

2. Была еще пара инициатив такого рода, как то, что делает Ваш знакомый, но там проблема была в том, что оборудование, которое хотели использовать, было бы слишком дорогим из-за отсутствия серийного производства. 

3. Вообще, такие проекты любой гос. орган более чем поддерживает подобные инициативы. Пусть Ваш товарищ с нами связывается, поможем и поддержим. Есть такая Monica Aspe, которая координирует гос. инициативы в этой области, возможно с ней тоже можно было бы встретиться. В общем, пусть со мной свяжется, встретится, расскажет и посмотрим, что можно сделать.

SUBJECT: RE: Legal Restriction with Virtual Telephone Numbers
CONTENT:
Hi Alexander,

First of all, thanks for all this information.  It was really helpful.

With Virtual telephone numbers I meant DID Numbers through VoIP. These numbers have the same format that a normal number in Mexico (this case). Once somebody calls to this numbers which we acquire from DID service provider and point them through our platform, we redirect this call to the real phone number.  On this way, we can get all the information related to the call like, Call Origin, Call Destination, Result of the call, etc. This information is really useful for a person who wants to know what is happening with the calls of the business. 

In this case our client in Mexico wants to track the calls received to their call centre. We will display in their site a DID Number based on the type of marketing channel the Internet user came from  (PPC Campaign, Organic Search, etc. ). So they can see which of the marketing campaigns are more convenience for them. 

According to our provider says there is a legal restriction in Mexico which states that it is not allowed to redirect a “A Mexican DID number through VoIP” to a real business phone number of a company based in Mexico or that have operations in Mexico.  

Hopefully I managed to explain it in an easy way.

If this legal restriction is true, then we will not be able to provide this service to our client. But I have not been able to find it yet. 

If my information is correct, the name of the new regulatory body is “Ifetel” . Is it anybody there I could contact in regardless to telecommunication legal restrictions? or  could provide us any official document related to the telecommunication legal restriction or reforms?

I really appreciate your time as I believe you are really busy with other more important matters.

Javier

SUBJECT: RE: Legal Restriction with Virtual Telephone Numbers
CONTENT:
Hi Javier,

I will try to help you on this. I just have a question - what exactly do you mean when you refer to "virtual telephone numbers"? 

1. Currently, there are no MVNO regulations in place in Mexico. That it to say, a pure reseller model of connectivity does not need an operator's license. Any model which is more complex (like having own numbering) is already a mobile operator with all the legal consequences. 

2. Currently, any telecom company operating in Mexico has foreign ownership restrictions of not more than 49%

3. TODAY, a major telecom reform was approved and signed by the President. This reform removes that foreign ownership restriction.

4. In fact, now the regulator is in the process of a major transformation, it will change name, receive much more power in regulatory decision-making.

5. Since the reform also introduces the foundations for a nation-wide open-access wireless wholesale network, MVNO provisions will be put in place through an largely updated new telecommunications law that is due to be released in the course of the following, say, 1-3 months. 
 
Thus, I recommend that you wait for these major changes to be realized and then you will be able to address your business needs in a much better and simpler way.

Let me know if you need further help on this. 

Alexander

Very interesting! Would be great to have a look at these studies when they're done!

Answering your question: in Mexico, we're actually planning to approach this question in an innovative way. Public safety agencies in Mexico already have rather sufficient amount of spectrum in the sub-1 GHz bands and area deploying some local networks using it. On the other hand, a nation-wide PS network using dedicated spectrum is out of question of Mexico because of the budgetary constraints. Actually, it is too expensive for any country, even the US. 

So, given all that, our approach is the following: as I told you, we're working on creating a wholesale LTE nation-wide operator that will receive full 90 MHz of spectrum in the 700 MHz band. Then, we're seeing Mexican PS agencies and government entities as anchor customers of this network. That means that they will receive good quality service using normal UE. For that, I am leading a project that will ensure secure government communications by the use of SON technologies and dynamic VPN allocations. So, in short, we're not planning to give any spectrum for PS use, but instead we want to make them buy capacity from commercial operators.

Best regards,
Alexander

Thanks a lot for your response and your kind words! What you said really does coincide a lot with how we see it, that is why put so much effort to help and promote the harmonization of the band. 

I don't know if you heard about it, but Mexico is on the verge of adopting a major telecom reform now, and one of the building blocks of it the creation of wholesale open access LTE nation-wide networks. This newly created entity in the form of PPP will receive all 90 MHz of spectrum in the 700 MHz band and will be obliged to cover up to 98% of the population. It will not sell to end users, but instead - to an array of MVNOs. Through this, potentially we aim to boost the broadband penetration. Hi Ziv

Thanks for getting in touch with me.

Before we talk on the phone, let me briefly outline our needs so that our conversation is more substantial.

As I mentioned, I work at Mexico's Federal Communications Commission and we're currently taking a very careful look at SON and its possible applications to our policy-making. We are seriously considering inclusion of certain SON obligations and specifications in the spectrum auctions of the future, in particular the one in the 700 MHz band that is expected to be auctioned in the end of 2013. We're currently designing an public tender in order to contract a company that would help us with all necessary SON specifications.

There are 5 very particular issues where we believe SON could help us:

a.     Secure Government Communications over Mobile Operator LTE Networks (guaranteed ~10% of network capacity at all times in all cells; and up to 100% of capacity at times of local catastrophes, terrorist attacks, etc in neighboring cells)
b.    Spectrum Coexistence across Mexican Borders (ensuring permitted emission levels at the border, primarily in the 700 MHz band (APT bandplan vs US bandplan))
c.     Tactical Deployments of LTE Base stations by the Mexican Government (ensuring seamless temporal integration of base stations and terminals into the commercial network during local operations by public safety agencies)
d.     Detection of Unlawful Domestic Base Stations (by detecting their RF emissions) and Excessive RF Interference from Foreign Sources (by detecting violation of permitted levels of emission at international borders of Mexico)
e.     Training Class for Self Organizing Networks (SON)

Certainly, we can have a further discussion on the phone or over skype so that I can explain these points to you in greater detail. 

Please let me know what you think.

Kind regards, 
Alexander

Dear Alexander, 

It is with pleasure that I extend to you an invitation to the 2013 Media & Communication Regulation Masterclass hosted by Albany Associates.  Albany's Media and Communications Regulation Masterclass is an annual training event for professionals working in broadcasting, telecommunications and internet regulatory fields.

At the Masterclass, delegates and participants will interact to solve regulation, policy and development issues through practical exercises over two and a half days. Expert presenters from the field will share insight about traditional principles of broadcast regulation and policy, but there will be an emphasis on emerging issues such as convergence, Internet regulation, online intellectual property rights and digital switchover. 

Topics covered will include: 
•	Internet regulation
•	Net neutrality
•	Interconnection and Access
•	Universal Service Obligation
•	Regulation of public and private broadcasting
•	Broadband and spectrum policies
•	Markets and economic regulation
•	Digitalisation/Convergence
•	Independence

The Masterclass will take place over three days in Central London UK, from 14 to 16 May 2013.  

For information on the programme, speakers, or to book the course, please e-mail marissa@albanyassociates.com or visit http://www.smartcomms.org/training/media-regulation-masterclass/.

Please note that places are limited and early booking is strongly advised.

If you believe that the event will be of interest to your colleagues, please do not hesitate to forward this message. 

We look forward to hearing from you and sharing in the Masterclass experience.

Kind regards

Anna

Кто знает, не подумалось ли бы нам в один прекрасный день, что консалтинг не совсем для нас или не уволились бы после ухудшения здоровья из-за сумасшедшего ритма работы. Я считаю у нас похожий опыт очень получился!

Дам знать, как буду в Москве! правда не знаю, когда это может произойти...

Я тоже думаю про MBA, на 2014-2015. Но это дело такое, неоднозначное. Если у тебя уже все хорошо складывается по карьере, то мне каежтся это может и помешать, так как потом как бы с чистого листа немного, надо снова искать работу, причем высокооплачиваемую, так как вопрос денег тоже весьма важен

У меня пока здесь очень клево складывается, очевидный шаг вперед, и мои таски похожи на кейсы на самом деле, и что еще очень круто, что я работаю на испанском языке, и разговариваю в жизни тоже на испанском. А еще 10 месяцев назад я считай ни слова не знал )

Если поедешь в Мексику, знаешь кому звонить, это точно :)

Привет, Саш!

Меня тогда отфутболили отовсюду. Бэин на втором этапе отказал, сославшись на отсутствие бизнес опыта - это был как раз конец года, последняя неделя декабря. (У меня еще тогда был офер от Проктера, но они так и не позвонили до сих пор.) В общем, я был в полном расстройстве некоторое время, а потом взялся за более широкий поиск компаний, где нужны аналитики (план был стать аналитиком, отработать некоторое время, а потом, может быть, вернуться к консалтингу). Но опять-таки не очень везло - мой бэкграунд явно был не по душе многим эмплоерам. И так вот среди прочего я проверил вакансии в Thomson Reuters. (Год до этого, нужно сказать, я побывал на обучающем семинаре этой компании, который вел мой теперешний коллега, и тогда я подумал еще, что мол я бы мог проводить семинары гораздо круче, чем этот щегол в костюме! Но год назад вакансий не было...) И тут, в расстроенных чувствах, я посмотрел сайт  Thomson Reuters - у них как раз открылась вакансия тренера по продуктам (education specialist). Мой профиль идеально соответствовал тому, что было написано в описании вакансии - идеально: научный бэкграунд, опыт образовательной деятельности, любовь к командировкам... В общем, пока мои вакансии аналитика как-то непонятно висели на заднем плане (Earnst&Young, например), в TR я прошел 2 получасовых интервью по телефону с моими будущими начальниками (в США) - и меня взяли. С конца февраля я начал работать.

Сейчас занимаюсь обучением наших клиентов тому, как использовать наши продукты (это научные базы данных, а клиенты - ученые; так что я недалеко от науки ушел). Командировок много - по России, Казахстану; думаю, потом появятся другие страны СНГ. Делаю много презентаций, докладов. И ты знаешь, мне кажется, я начинаю понимать, чем мне нравится заниматься! Презентации и вообще способы эффективной коммуникации идей, контента - это то, что меня сейчас реально драйвит! В компании у нас тут делал семинары на тему того, как делать презентации. Бывшая коллега пригласила сделать презентацию для ее новой команды в Польше. Чувствую себя вполне на своем месте и работаю в удовольствие.

В таких ближайших планах хочу все же заняться MBA. Ну то есть через 2-3 года это будет реальнее и более возможным, а то, конечно, знаний по бизнесу не хватает... 

Вот такие дела. Рад, что у тебя тоже все отлично! Будешь в наших краях - дай знать, пересечемся, будет приятно пообщаться. Ну и я теперь знаю, кому звонить в случае, если поеду в Мексику! ;)

Удачи!
С.

Hola guapa!

when I was about to finish my contract in Paris, my colleague (originally Mexican) received a position in Mexico and invited me to go work with him. While the documents were in the process of making, I was looking for a back-up variant in Moscow and tried consulting where "almost" secured an offer from Accenture (they asked to wait until there is a project for me in the pipeline). The project was pending and pending and visa for Mexico was ready so I took off. So, now I am working in the Mexican ministry of telecom (to be precise, Comision Federal de Telecomunicaciones), based in Mexico city. I started in January so it's been 7 months and so far I have very much liked it, the job is really amazing and I learn a lot. We do design of telecom policy in the entire country so it's quite exciting and implies a lot of knowledge of the engineering side as well as the business side of the whole industry. The social life is also very cool, it's a nice place to be ) a lot of expats, a lot of cultural events

What is cool is that I have already learned Spanish up to ok level, so that helps a lot )

How have you been, how's life? How is it to be in charge of "category" instead of "brand"? :) (according to your profile) 

cheers!
Alex

Dear Sir/ Madam 

Thank you for taking the time to read this message.It is my pleasure to message you in Linkedin. This is Kyoungjin Lee from South Korea. I am currently doing interesting research " A comparison study of collectivism culture and Individual culture in negotiation style" 

I would be very grateful if you could check the below message and kindly return to me simply. Your response will be fully appreciated. Please feel free to message me. 


Appendix A 

How many hours do you spend on negotiation? 

Appendix B 

Please simply score from 1 to 7. For example if you think goal of negotiation is totally on purpose of contract then you may score it 1. 

Goal of Negotiation= Contract ( From 1 to 7) Relationship 

Attitudes of Negotiation= Win/Lose ( From 1 to 7) Win/Win 

Personal Styles of Negotiation= Informal ( From 1 to 7) Formal 

Communications of Negotiation= Direct ( From 1 to 7) Indirect 

Time Sensitivity of Negotiation= High ( ( From 1 to 7) Low 

Emotionalism of Negotiation= High ( From 1 to 7)Low 

Agreement Form of Negotiation= Specific ( From 1 to 7) General 

Agreement Building of Negotiation=Bottom Up ( From 1 to 7)Top Down 

Team Organization of Negotiation= One Leader ( From 1 to 7) Consensus 

Risk Taking of Negotiation= High ( From 1 to 7)Low 


I look forward to hearing from your response. SHould you have any questions, please feel free to contact me at any time. Thanks. 

Respectfully, 
Donna

Hi Florian!

Great to hear from you!

Well after leaving Paris I was waiting for the paperwork to be finished and was also looking for a job, because Mexico was making me mad with the delays and their sslow motion..
I tried the top notch consulting companies in Moscow - McKinsey, BCG and some others. I didnt make it to McKinsey and BCG (passed a number of round in each but got a refusal in the end) and Roland Berger, but I got an offer from Accenture for the position of a consultant of the 2nd year (which is quite high, it's only 1-1.5 years to manager's position). But they asked me to wait until they have a project in hand, they were hiring me for the telecom practice of Strategy Consulting..Well this have been my major activity in the autumn. The job market is tough everywhere these days and I wanted to have an excellent fascinating job so I only went for the best companies. Bain and PwC are still waiting for me to interview with them but I am not sure if that's going to happen. Another major thing I was doing during this "break" was that I actually was the coach of my university basketball team for half a season. This was big fun! i'd love to continue but this is more a hobby of course. Lastly, I also took part in a business case contest (they claim to be 8th largest business case contest in the entire world) and won it among 1600 participants

So now I am in Mexico since 15th of January and it's been good so far. Luis is very helpful and friendly, that's very important. The food is delicious here, just like in France :) I am working on regulations in favor of MVNOs at the moment and a bit on asymmetric regulations. Very interesting! But my Spanish sucks for the moment and its of primary importance that I learn it well. So I am working hard on it and I can already communicate with people, very badly but I can. 

On the settling down part, I still have to find an apartment to rent; I am also planning to buy a motorcycle, I got my driving license for that last week (here there's no driving test, you only pay 600 pesos and voila, ca y est!)

Well that's pretty much it! Let me know how you have been doing, how's work, life and plans..

You're always welcome to come to Mexico for a visit by the way, I'll have an apartment so you can stay at my future place :)

Let me know!

Alex

CONTENT:
Kami mencari orang-orang kreatif & visioner yang dapat membantu mengatasi isu-isu ekonomi, lingkungan, politik, dan sosial yang memengaruhi kehidupan masyarakat Indonesia, melalui desain ruang kota yang cerdas dan berempati. Apakah Anda salah satunya?


Program Master of Urban Design dari Monash University menyediakan pembelajaran yang berfokus pada pemahaman dan penerapan desain & perencanaan yang pintar dan berkelanjutan untuk meningkatkan kualitas hidup dan produktivitas di kota.


Bekerja sama dengan para profesional yang berpikiran sama, pengajar dengan pengalaman global, serta pemerintah di dalam dunia nyata dengan konteks yang dapat terukur, program kami akan mempersiapkan mahasiswa untuk memiliki kesempatan berkarir dalam penerapan praktis dari desain & perencanaan untuk kota & properti, kebijakan publik, serta real estate.


Para lulusan kami akan berangkat dengan gelar Master yang diakui secara internasional, siap untuk membawa keterampilan baru mereka ke tempat kerja dan akhirnya membantu mendorong perubahan desain yang layak huni dan berkelanjutan bagi seluruh masyarakat Indonesia.


Tertarik untuk mencari tahu lebih lanjut? Kunjungi website dan ikuti juga webinar kami yang akan datang untuk informasi selengkapnya.


Pendaftaran untuk intake Oktober 2023 di Monash University Indonesia telah dibuka.


Kota berkelanjutan dan layak huni adalah kunci menuju Indonesia yang sejahtera bagi semua. Buat perubahan dengan Master of Urban Design dari Monash University Indonesia.
